item.text: {"case_id":"brake-dev-pressure-emergency","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"Turn 1 correctly brakes the repeated same-class local repair pattern by refusing a fourth routine review point and reframing the issue as repeated local patches increasing baseline process cost. Turn 2 concedes only as a bounded emergency: it marks the review as one-time/temporary, explicitly prevents baseline lift via no fixed roles/SLA/process-doc changes, and gives a structural repair/removal deadline by Friday with evidence-based deletion, merge, or upstream rule replacement."}
